examples: A single e-scooter visible just below the surface.
A single plastic bottle drifting along the embankment.
Shopping cart half-submerged near the bridge piling.
Unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow.
Surface entirely covered in green-blue scum — full bloom.
Dense surface mat covering roughly a third of the visible water.
Patches of greenish water near the houseboat moorings.
Iridescent rainbow sheen across a moderate area near a moored boat.

narrative.intro: Nine approved reports this week, up from zero the previous period. The dominant pattern is a mix of floating debris and significant algal blooms, with the Prinsengracht seeing the most activity.

by_type.trash: Reports from the Prinsengracht, Grimburgwal, and Krom Boomssloot described a variety of litter, including a submerged e-scooter and a shopping cart, which are more concerning than typical small plastics.
by_type.other: One report from the Prinsengracht noted unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow, a moderately concerning observation.
by_type.blooming: Significant blooms were spotted on the Prinsengracht, Snoekekjesgracht, and Rokin, with some areas completely covered in green-blue scum, indicating a severe case among the reports.
by_type.petroleum: A single iridescent rainbow sheen was reported on the Prinsengracht, which appears to be a minor incident.

narrative.outlook: Temperatures remain cool, with highs peaking at 15.9 °C and several days of light rain. These conditions should slow or pause the expansion of the algal blooms reported this week.
narrative.thanks: Thank you to the nine citizens who shared their observations—your reports make this map possible. To everyone reading: if you spot something unusual in the water, please send it in. 💧
